definition.description: Pacific Coast Denial Defense — Appeal Package Builder. Assembles a structured Medicare appeal package from the Skilled Justification Builder output. Combines patient data, clinical note, SNJ statement, and regulatory citations into a formatted appeal-ready document. Grounded in CMS Appeals Process (Redetermination, QIC, ALJ), Jimmo v. Sebelius, and Microsoft Learn Power Automate document assembly best practices.

text.description: The Skilled Justification statement generated by the SNJ Builder
text.title: SkilledJustificationText

text_2.description: Discipline and document type (e.g., PT Progress Note)
text_2.title: DisciplineAndDocType

text_3.description: Denial reason stated by payer (e.g., 'Lack of Medical Necessity')
text_3.title: DenialReason

inputs.AppealPackage:
@{concat('=== PACIFIC COAST THERAPY HUB — MEDICARE APPEAL PACKAGE ===
Appeal ID: ', variables('AppealID'), '
Date: ', utcNow(), '

SECTION 1: PATIENT INFORMATION
Patient Alias: ', triggerBody()?['text_1'], '
Discipline / Document Type: ', triggerBody()?['text_2'], '
Clinical Diagnosis: ', triggerBody()?['text_4'], '

SECTION 2: DENIAL INFORMATION
Denial Reason Stated: ', triggerBody()?['text_3'], '

SECTION 3: SKILLED JUSTIFICATION REBUTTAL
', triggerBody()?['text'], '

SECTION 4: REGULATORY AUTHORITY CITATIONS
1. Jimmo v. Sebelius (2013) Settlement Agreement: Medicare coverage is NOT limited to patients who demonstrate potential for improvement. Coverage extends to maintenance therapy when a licensed therapist is required to maintain function or prevent further decline.
2. CMS Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 15, Section 220/230/240: Skilled therapy services are covered when the inherent complexity of the service, condition of the patient, or both require the judgment, knowledge, and skills of a qualified therapist.
3. APTA Clinical Practice Guidelines: Evidence-based functional outcomes supporting skilled PT intervention.
4. AOTA Occupational Therapy Practice Framework (4th Ed.): Skilled OT services grounded in occupational profile and performance analysis.
5. ASHA Evidence Maps: Research-supported SLP interventions for the documented condition.
6. 42 CFR 409.32 — SNF Level of Care Requirements: Patient requires skilled care that can only be provided safely and effectively by or under the supervision of licensed professionals.

SECTION 5: APPEAL PATHWAY
Step 1 — Redetermination: Submit to Medicare Administrative Contractor (MAC) within 120 days of denial date.
Step 2 — QIC Reconsideration: If Redetermination denied, submit to Qualified Independent Contractor within 180 days.
Step 3 — ALJ Hearing: If QIC denied and amount in controversy exceeds threshold, request ALJ hearing within 60 days.

SECTION 6: SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION CHECKLIST
[ ] Complete clinical note(s) for the denial period
[ ] Physician certification/recertification
[ ] Plan of Care with active goals
[ ] Prior level of function documentation
[ ] Standardized assessment results
[ ] IDT meeting notes (if applicable)
[ ] Any additional clinical correspondence

=== END APPEAL PACKAGE ===
*Prepared by Pacific Coast Therapy Hub Denial Defense Engine. Grounded in Jimmo v. Sebelius, CMS MBPM Chapter 15, APTA/AOTA/ASHA standards. All content requires attorney/compliance officer review before submission.*')}
